message:
fix(core,vdbe,e2e): flush retained autocommit on explicit BEGIN, add prepared-statement oracle tests and reuse-state optimization validation

Addresses a correctness edge case where a retained autocommit
transaction could leak into a subsequent explicit BEGIN, and expands
test coverage for lazy memdb mirroring and execution state reuse.

- Guard explicit BEGIN processing to detect and flush any existing
  retained autocommit transaction before starting the new transaction,
  preventing stale state leakage across transaction boundaries
- Refactor retained autocommit oracle test to use prepared statements
  with bound SqliteValue::Integer parameters instead of inline SQL,
  exercising the prepared-statement path through retained autocommit
- Add PRAGMA fsqlite.concurrent_mode = OFF in autocommit_txn_tests
  to isolate retained autocommit behavior from concurrent mode
- Add correctness_mixed_dml e2e test: 120-row mixed DML (inserts,
  updates every 3rd row, deletes every 5th) with periodic count/value
  queries validated against csqlite oracle with state hash comparison
- Add correctness_sequential_insert e2e test: 10k sequential inserts
  with periodic COUNT/SELECT verification at every 2,500th row,
  asserting zero mismatches and matching logical state hashes
- Add VDBE test for reusable table execution state: verify
  apply_reusable_table_execution_state skips redundant metadata
  rebinding after reset_for_reuse_preserving_runtime_setup but
  correctly rebinds after full reset_for_reuse
- Update legacy SQLite submodule pointer

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
